questions.prompt: Which statement best describes the law of demand?

options.content: When price increases, demand decreases.
When price increases, demand increases.
Price and quantity demanded are inversely related.
Price and quantity demanded are directly related.
The demand curve is always upward sloping.

questions.prompt: GDP is an imperfect measure of a nation’s economic performance because it does not account for which of the following?

options.content: Purchases made by the nation’s government
Purchases of domestically produced goods by international consumers

Goods produced at home for personal consumption
Personal consumption expenditures on durable goods

questions.prompt: If France has a comparative advantage in the production of grapes, and Italy has an absolute advantage in the production of olives, which of the following statements must be true?

options.content: France and Italy will not benefit from trading with each other.
France can produce more olives than Italy can.
France can produce more grapes than Italy can.
France has a lower opportunity cost in producing grapes than Italy does.
France has a higher opportunity cost in producing grapes than Italy does.

questions.prompt: Assuming the marginal propensity to consume is less than 1, which of the following describes why the magnitude of the tax multiplier is smaller than the magnitude of the expenditure multiplier?

options.content: Changes in income taxes initially affect both household consumption and household savings.
Changes in income taxes initially affect household savings only.
Changes in income taxes initially affect net investment instead of consumption.
Changes in income taxes initially affect exports only.
Changes in income taxes initially affect firms only.

questions.prompt: Which of the following best represents an investment in human capital?

options.content: A clothing manufacturer purchases automated machinery.

A bank pays the tuition for one of its managers to pursue a degree in business administration.
A paper mill offers a bonus to an employee who develops a more efficient way to incentivize workers to work more night shifts.

questions.prompt: If the inflation rate increases, what will happen to the real interest rate on existing loans with fixed nominal interest rates?

options.content: It will increase.
It will decrease.
It will stay the same.
It will be greater than the nominal interest rate.
It will equal the unexpected inflation rate minus the expected inflation rate.

questions.prompt: Assume that a nation’s capital and financial account is in balance. If the nation’s central bank implements contractionary monetary policy, which of the following will occur solely as a result of the change in the nation’s interest rates?

options.content: There will be an increase in financial capital outflows.
The balance of payments will be greater than zero.
Both the current account and the capital and financial account will move into deficit.
International investors will buy less of the nation’s financial assets.
The capital and financial account will move into surplus.

questions.prompt:
The economy of Country X is experiencing demand-pull inflation as a result of an increase in consumer spending. If the government and central bank of Country X want to reduce inflation and the banking system of Country X has ample reserves, which of the following combinations of fiscal and monetary policy would be most effective to achieve their goal in the short run?

Fiscal Policy ActionMonetary Policy Action
ADecrease government spendingIncrease administered interest rates
BDecrease government spendingDecrease administered interest rates
CIncrease income taxesDecrease administered interest rates
DDecrease income taxesIncrease administered interest rates
EIncrease transfer paymentsIncrease administered interest rates

options.content: A
B
C
D
E

questions.prompt: Assume an economy is in long-run equilibrium. If there is an increase in short-run aggregate supply, how will it be demonstrated in the Phillips curve model?
